interactions.summary: Jiang defines transnational capital as a wealthy class with loyalty only to capital, then identifies secret societies as the trust infrastructure that lets such capital move across places.
He contrasts peasant religion with elite religion and argues that the lender class requires a ritual system that makes terrible acts psychologically possible.

He uses the Milgram experiment to explain authority and responsibility-displacement, then maps that onto secret societies praying to Satan or Saturn so members experience themselves as instruments rather than agents.

claims.claim: Transnational capital has no loyalty to nation, people, or place; it moves wherever capital is safer, better protected, and more profitable.
Secret societies and transnational capital are essentially the same force because secret societies provide trust, coordination, and shared worldview across places.
Peasant religion teaches ordinary moral life, while the lender class needs a religious practice that permits control, extraction, and evil acts.
claims.temporal_scope: broad civilizational model in the lecture

claims.claim: The Milgram experiment shows, for Jiang's purposes, that people can inflict pain when responsibility is transferred to an authority figure.
Secret society ritual displaces agency onto a higher power such as Satan or Saturn, allowing members to believe that evil acts serve order or paradise.
claims.temporal_scope: Jiang's model of elite ritual psychology

signature_moments.moment: The ruling class has no homeland, only capital.
signature_moments.source_phrase: no loyalty to nation, or to people, or to place
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This phrase condenses the move from local landlord to mobile global power.

signature_moments.moment: Secret societies are presented as the concrete mechanism that lets capital travel.
signature_moments.source_phrase: secret societies and transnational capital are essentially the same thing
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the lecture's title-level thesis.

signature_moments.moment: Evil is outsourced to a god: 'It was Satan who did this.'
signature_moments.source_phrase: It was Satan that told us to do it
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This turns occult ritual into a psychology of plausible deniability.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A mobile wealthy class loyal to capital rather than nation, people, or place.
In this lecture, the trust and ritual structure that allows transnational capitalists to coordinate and act across locations.
A mechanism by which people do harmful acts by believing an authority or higher power owns the action.

uncertainty_notes.note: The transcript has repeated 'in order' fragments at the segment end; the Saturn/Kronos model is clear, but the final wording should not be over-quoted.
